the best traffic for you, your product and your business. However, since this is a brand

the best cost per conversion for your product and your services. That's why we want to
start off with product and brand consideration, because there is no data. It is a fresh
account. This is always the best option to start gathering data, seeing what works,
before switching over to sales and leads, which I'll get into in later on this video.

Speaker 1: Frequency capping is very huge, because let's say you're showing your video ad. You
don't want to show the same video ad to the same person over and over and over and
over and over and over and over again, and them still not buy but then just get mad and
not like you, right? So, the way to avoid that is to enter a frequency capping and an
impressions cap and a view cap, right?
Speaker 1: So, how you do that is, let's say you only want to show your video to the same person
seven times per week in this campaign. This means that an impression is someone who
gets impressed with your video, they see a few seconds, but then they click skip. So,
you're not charged for that person viewing your video because they didn't view it yet.
Speaker 1: A view only costs you when they watch 30 seconds or they click on your video. So,
showing a video ad to the same person seven times without them viewing it is a pretty
good number to start off with per week. That's what I start off with. And I go ahead an
do three views per week as well. So, they're only allowed to watch the video three times
in a week span, which means I won't get charged more than them watching that video
three times per week, which is good, because if they watch it three times in a week, the
same video ad, then, uh, it's a good balance where they're not gonna get pissed off, but
they're also going to have a good interaction with my business and my brand.

know, what's, what, what they are as a gender."
